This classification reflects the unique characteristics of the plant, including its simple leaf structure and the absence of true roots and leaves. Overall, the classification of Lycopodioides pteryphyllos highlights its distinctive features and relationships to other vascular plants.

Lycopodium clavatum, commonly known as Club Moss, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the Lycopodiaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce pain, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis. The bioactive constituents of Lycopodium clavatum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
